schema.appDescription: Compare your current home loan EMI to a refinanced offer. See exact savings from balance transfer or repo rate reduction.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Mortgage Refinance Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option for your situation.

faq.q: How accurate is the Mortgage Refinance Calculator?
faq.a: The Mortgage Refinance Calculator uses standard financial formulas. Results are indicative; actual figures depend on your lender or financial institution's policies.

faq.q: Is the Mortgage Refinance Calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, the Mortgage Refinance Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We do not store any of your financial data.

faq.q: How often are the Mortgage Refinance Calculator rates updated?
faq.a: Rates in examples are updated periodically to reflect current market conditions. Always verify with your bank or advisor.

faq.q: Can I use the Mortgage Refinance Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised. The calculator works on all modern smartphones and tablets.
Anyone in India planning their finances can benefit from the Mortgage Refinance Calculator. It's designed for both beginners and experienced investors.

examples.heading: ₹20 lakh mortgage refinance at 8.5% for 20 years
examples.text: A ₹20 lakh mortgage refinance at 8.5% p.a. over 20 years results in an EMI of approximately ₹17,357. Total interest paid over the term is ₹21.66 lakh — making total cost ₹41.66 lakh.

examples.heading: ₹50 lakh mortgage refinance at 9% for 15 years
examples.text: Borrowing ₹50 lakh at 9% for 15 years gives an EMI of ₹50,713. Total interest is ₹41.28 lakh. Reducing tenure to 10 years cuts total interest to ₹26.03 lakh, saving ₹15.25 lakh.
